title: Introduction to Spinoza’s Ethics

weeks.note: Seminarraum 4 der Jüdischen Hochschule, Landfriedstraße 12 69117 Heidelberg
Seminarraum 4 der Jüdischen Hochschule, Landfriedstraße 12 69117 Heidelberg

further_info: Recommended Reading Bennett, Jonathan. A Studz of Spinoza's Ethics. Indianapolis: Hackett, 1984. Douglas, Alexander. Spinoza and Dutch Cartesianism. Oxford–New York: Oxford University Press, 2015. Garrett, Don (ed.). The Cambridge Companion to Spinoza (2nd edition). Cambridge: Cambridge University Press, 2022. James, Susan. Spinoza on Learning to Live Together. Oxford–New York: Oxford University Press, 2020. Kisner, Matthew–Youpa, Andrew (eds.). Essays on Spinoza's Ethical Theory. Oxford–New York: Oxford University Press, 2014. Koistinen, Olli (ed.). The Cambridge Companion to Spinoza's Ethics. New York: Cambridge University Press, 2009. LeBuffe, Michael. Spinoza's Ethics. A Guide. Oxford-New York: Oxford University Press, 2023. Melamed, Yitzhak Y. (ed.). A Companion to Spinoza. Hoboken, NJ.: Wiley, 2021. Melamed,Yitzhak Y. Spinoza's Metaphysics: Substance and Thought. New York: Oxford University Press, 2013. Naaman-Zauderer, Noa (ed.). Freedom, Action, and Motivation in Spinoza's "Ethics". New York: Routledge, 2019. Nadler, Steven. Spinoza's Ethics: An Introduction. Cambridge: Cambridge University Press: 2009. Nadler, Steven. Spinoza's Hereesy: Immortality and the Jewish Mind. Oxford: Oxford University Press, 2001. Sangiacomo, Andrea. Spinoza's Yoga: Practice of Power and Experience of the Infinite. Groningen: University of Groningen Press, 2025. Wolfson, Harry Austryn. The Philosophy of Spinoza: Unfolding the Latent Process of His Reasoning. Cambridge, Mass.: Harvard University Press, 1983. Youpa, Andrew. The Ethics of Joy: Spinoza on the Empowered Life. Oxford–New York: Oxford University Press, 2020.
